subject: lonely retirees
one_liner: Retiree loneliness is, at its core, a trajectory problem. Perigee launches them toward each other.
brand_voice_notes: Deadpan aerospace flight-ops. Treats a lonely retiree as a crewed mission: flight plan, telemetry, a certified flight director, and, for the ones cleared to stay, a companionship habitat in low Earth orbit. Every human feeling is restated as an orbital-mechanics quantity. Never warm, never winking. The numbers are always confident and always slightly wrong for the situation.

hero.h1: Your father is not lonely. He is between launch windows.
hero.sub: Perigee runs each retiree as a crewed mission. Flight plan, live telemetry, a certified flight director, and a berth aboard a companionship habitat at 420 km for the ones cleared to stay up there.

features.title: Contact Window Optimization
features.blurb: Our guidance computer models when your daughter is statistically most likely to answer and opens a 90-second window. Burn it or hold for the next one, currently 47 days out.

features.title: Mandatory GO / NO-GO Poll
features.blurb: Before any call home, your assigned flight director reads a 22-item pre-call checklist and polls the room. A single NO-GO and the call is scrubbed. This is a safety feature.

features.title: Sundowner Station
features.blurb: Retirees cleared for Trans-Colony Injection relocate to a pressurized companionship module at 420 km with 39 age-matched crewmates and no scheduled de-orbit. Loneliness, solved by relocation.

pricing_tiers.name: Suborbital
pricing_tiers.tagline: You go up. You come back down. Nothing about the house changes.
perks: One parabolic contact window per month
Ground-based flight director, shared across 40 retirees
Guaranteed return to the same empty living room

pricing_tiers.name: Low Earth Orbit
perks: Weekly contact windows on a priority uplink
A flight director assigned to your loneliness specifically
Full telemetry shared with next of kin whether they asked or not

pricing_tiers.name: Trans-Colony Injection
pricing_tiers.tagline: The last address you will ever need to update.
perks: No de-orbit vehicle on the manifest, by design

testimonials.quote: My flight director scrubbed three of my calls to my son. He says the telemetry on the fourth one looks strong.
testimonials.name: Harold Vessey, 74
testimonials.title: Payload, Mission LEO-118

testimonials.quote: I have not been this close to other people since basic. We are 420 kilometers up and I cannot get away from a single one of them.
testimonials.name: Diane Pruett, 71
testimonials.title: Berth 12, Sundowner Station

testimonials.quote: They told me the loneliness was a fuel problem. Fourteen launches later I have come to feel that was not the problem.
testimonials.name: Walter Kominski, 79
testimonials.title: Suborbital tier, still on the pad

demo_screen_concept: A flight-operations console tracking a fleet of retiree missions. A big board of mission cards, each with a call sign, retiree name, a mission-phase pill (ON PAD / ASCENT / ON STATION), telemetry rows for steps, meds, and last family contact, and a live T-minus countdown to the next contact window. A left rail filters the fleet by phase, a GO / NO-GO poll widget sits in a header strip, each card has a HOLD / RESUME control, and clicking a mission opens a telemetry detail modal. Blueprint-navy instrument styling on drafting-paper ground, launch-orange reserved for holds and alerts.

images.rationale: Typographic blueprint hero, skipped (technical-manual style earns no photo). Demo is a flight-ops console, skipped (terminal/telemetry demos get no imagery). Full budget spent on three testimonial headshots, staged as deadpan official crew-ID portraits of the retiree users, which harmonize with the drafting-navy palette and sell the bit.

items.prompt: Official crew identification portrait, editorial photograph. A single elderly man around 74, thin combed-back grey hair, lined weary face, calm deadpan neutral expression, plain slate-grey collared coverall, warm drafting-grey backdrop, muted navy/off-white grade. Hard bans: no text/letters/numbers/logos/insignia/badges/watermarks/UI.

Official crew identification portrait, editorial photograph. A single elderly woman around 71, short neat silver hair, composed steady deadpan neutral expression, plain slate-grey collared coverall, warm drafting-grey backdrop, muted navy/off-white grade. Hard bans: no text/letters/numbers/logos/insignia/badges/watermarks/UI.

Official crew identification portrait, editorial photograph. A single elderly man around 79, bald with wire-frame glasses, tired resigned deadpan neutral expression, plain slate-grey collared coverall, warm drafting-grey backdrop, muted navy/off-white grade. Hard bans: no text/letters/numbers/logos/insignia/badges/watermarks/UI.

design_direction.style: Retro-futurist aerospace technical-manual, drafting-paper daylight mode. The page is staged as the flight-operations manual of a heavily funded launch provider that has decided retiree loneliness is an orbital-mechanics problem and prices companionship in delta-v. A warm drafting off-white ground carries faint blueprint grid ticks along section edges and thin leader lines with small round terminators, so the whole page reads as an engineering plate rather than a website. Panels are flat instrument plates: 1px navy hairline borders, 4px radii, small corner part-numbers (P/N PRG-002) and revision chips, no glass, no blur, no offset shadows. One blueprint navy carries the wordmark, headlines, structural hairlines, leader lines, and every institutional surface; one rationed launch orange, the color of a range-safety stripe, is reserved strictly for the single primary CTA per section, the live status lamp, HOLD and abort affordances, and hazard-striped dividers, and appears almost nowhere else. Space Grotesk sets the wordmark and headlines in technical geometric caps with the confidence of a mission patch; Inter carries calm, procedural spec-sheet body copy. Every figure that pretends to be flight telemetry (contact-window countdowns, apoapsis, crew counts, tier prices, GO/NO-GO tallies, altitudes) is set in B612 Mono, the typeface Airbus drew for cockpit displays, with tabular figures inside ruled label wells, so it reads like an instrument readout, not marketing. Motion is instrument-panel restrained: the hero trajectory arc draws itself once on load, the status lamp pulses on a 3s cycle, plates rise 6px and fade in on scroll, the CTA depresses 1px, the demo countdown ticks, all gated behind prefers-reduced-motion. No dark mode, no neon, no glassmorphism, no clay, no serifs, no bento lattice, no marquee, no scanlines. Reads like a company that files an old man's Tuesday under 'mission ops' and lists his estrangement from his kids as an unresolved trajectory anomaly.
design_direction.archetype: Aerospace launch-provider landing, SpaceX-adjacent, staged as a flight-operations manual: slim wordmark nav with a status lamp, split hero with a technical claim left and a blueprint trajectory plate right (Earth curve, launch arc, orbital companionship habitat, dimension lines labeling CONTACT WINDOW and APOAPSIS), features as three numbered flight-system callouts, testimonials as crew logs with headshots, pricing as three launch classes, a hazard-striped orange CTA band, and a handoff to the flight-ops demo console.
design_direction.motion_vocab: Instrument-panel restraint: trajectory arc draws once on load, status lamp pulses on a 3s cycle, plates rise 6px and fade on scroll, CTA depresses 1px, countdowns tick in the demo, all gated behind prefers-reduced-motion.
design_direction.hero_composition_notes: Left column: small-caps eyebrow 'FLIGHT SYSTEM / P/N PRG-001', a tight technical H1, procedural subhead, one orange CTA plus a ghost 'read the flight plan' link, and a mono telemetry strip along the bottom (T-MINUS, APOAPSIS, CREW). Right column: a flat CSS/SVG blueprint plate on navy hairlines showing an Earth-limb curve, a launch trajectory arc rising to a small orbital habitat glyph, with leader lines and dimension callouts labeling CONTACT WINDOW, APOAPSIS: COMPANIONSHIP, and DELTA-V BUDGET, all in B612 Mono.
